pending.body: Stop canonicalizing the claimed bead ID unless the rest of the controller/runtime normalizes IDs the same way. The selection path now rewrites a ready bead like `bead-x` to `ms-id.bead-x` before storing it in controller state (`src/cli/run.rs:272-279`, `src/cli/run.rs:588-596`), but later execution paths still compare bead IDs as raw strings (`src/cli/run.rs:768-785`, `src/cli/run.rs:1018-1023`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:1339-1348`). For milestones that use explicit short bead IDs, selecting the next bead can therefore leave the controller tracking `ms-id.bead-x` while the project/task source still carries `bead-x`; the next start/resume/sync then fails with “controller is already tracking active bead ... and cannot adopt ...”. Either preserve the original `br` bead ID here or switch the downstream alignment checks to the same normalized/bead-match comparison.
